newsSince: No genuinely new fundamental news since June 9. The Q2 2026 beat (May 7), raised FY26 datacenter revenue (>60% growth), strong Q3 guide and early-June IMS SATCOM/optical launches were all in the prior cases. June sell-side momentum continued (Truist to $375, Evercore to $427), but these are PT moves, not thesis-changing events. The +3.8% move is price.

sources.label: MACOM Q2 2026 earnings transcript (Motley Fool, May 7)
MACOM Q2 2026 beat on AI optical demand (Sahm Capital, June 4)

verdicts.rankRationale: Passives/power lens: MACOM is interconnect/optical, farther from the passive-power bottleneck stack. AI optical strength is real but orthogonal to this page; nothing new since June 9 touches passives, so hold in the monitor bucket at 66.
verdicts.bull: Datacenter optical demand >60% FY26 growth shows real AI pull, even if not a passives name.
verdicts.neutral: Strong AI exposure but sits adjacent to, not inside, the passive/power bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Wrong page for the thesis; valuation rich after +113% YTD with no passives angle.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent connectivity/photonics: Q2 beat plus raised >60% FY26 datacenter growth and a strong Q3 guide reinforce the optical/datacom leg, but it was already in the June 9 mark. Modest +1 score nod, no rank move at 37.
verdicts.bull: Optical/datacom and defense-RF demand compounding; raised FY26 datacenter outlook and margin expansion toward 60% gross.
verdicts.neutral: Strong fundamentals already reflected after a 113% YTD run; less AI crowding than mega-cap semis.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical RF/industrial mix and rich multiple leave little latent edge.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Datacenter optical revenue >60% FY26 growth; Q3 guide above consensus.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression after a sharp run; RF/industrial cyclicality.

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: analog/power/RF tier, less direct than HBM/advanced-packaging/AI-networking. June 9 already moved it up on AI-connectivity proximity; nothing new since bears on the compute bottleneck, so hold at 56.
verdicts.bull: AI optical/connectivity exposure with datacenter revenue >60% FY26 growth gives genuine AI-node adjacency.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI pull but indirect versus core HBM/packaging/networking bottleneck nodes.
verdicts.bear: Analog/power/RF tier is a secondary node; less leverage to the acute compute bottleneck.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Datacenter optical ramp deepening AI-node relevance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Stays a peripheral node vs primary AI-compute chokepoints.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO/optics lens: rank 2 on a crowding-sensitive list. 200G/lane interconnect thesis intact and the Q2 beat confirms AI optical pull, but growing sell-side attention (PTs to $375/$427/$450) shrinks residual alpha; offsets net to a hold.
verdicts.bull: Strong 200G/lane optical/copper interconnect exposure with a smaller cap than crowded optics winners; AI optical beat validates the lens.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but sell-side crowding (multiple PT hikes) is compressing the residual edge that earns rank 2.
verdicts.bear: Crowding rising and stock up 113% YTD; CPO alpha thinning, execution/cyclical risk remains.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 200G/lane CPO design wins and continued AI optical ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding erodes alpha; cyclical RF/industrial and execution risk.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master list / EDA-test-timing bucket: design-validation framing is a loose fit. June 9 already down-ranked for the YTD rerating; no new design-validation/test news since, so hold at 80.
verdicts.bull: AI optical and connectivity strength supports the broad bottleneck-exposure case.
verdicts.neutral: Bucketed under EDA/test/timing where MACOM is only a tangential fit; fundamentals fine but already priced.
verdicts.bear: Rich after 113% YTD; weak fit to the design-validation bucket caps ranking.
verdicts.primaryRisk: YTD rerating leaves limited residual upside on this list.
